Ad Text:
We're partnering with a leading financial services organisation on a major finance transformation program, building a Finance Data Hub and driving best practice across ERP and feeder systems.

This is a high-impact, hands-on advisory role for a senior professional who can challenge the status quo, define finance data solutions, and influence the target-state design. You'll work with Finance, Data and Technology to ensure a scalable, accurate, and future-proof finance data landscape.

Key responsibilities will include:

Defining finance data requirements across GL, regulatory and management reporting.
Collaborating with the Data Office and technical teams to ensure data standards, dictionaries and governance are implemented.
Challenging legacy processes and proposing innovative, practical improvements.
Shaping the target-state finance data solutions to support reporting and transformation goals.
Partnering with stakeholders across Finance, Data and Technology to translate requirements into actionable solutions.
Focusing on future-state outcomes - ensuring finance data is structured, auditable, and fit-for-purpose.

To succeed, you will offer:

Strong finance and ERP system experience with exposure to data governance.
Understanding of finance data flows and reporting requirements.
Experience in finance transformation programs or large-scale change initiatives.
Ability to influence stakeholders, challenge assumptions, and implement best practice.
Excellent communication and problem-solving skills.
General Insurance / IFRS 17 experience is a plus, but not essential.

This is a chance to shape finance data strategy, influence ERP and feeder systems, and help define a golden source of truth for finance - all while driving meaningful transformation across the organisation.

Contact Nick Largent for more information.

Company: Department of Sport, Racing and Olympic and Paralympic Games
Ad Text:
In this role, you will:
• Design, implement, and maintain a comprehensive reporting and governance framework to support the oversight of Brisbane 2032 Games delivery and legacy outcomes.
• Develop high-quality reports, briefings, and dashboards for senior executives, governance groups, and stakeholders, leveraging digital tools and data analytics to ensure clarity and precision.
• Provide expert advice and guidance on governance best practices, compliance, and reporting requirements to support the effective delivery of the Games.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams within OPGO and across local, state, and federal government agencies, as well as the Brisbane 2032 Organising Committee (OCOG) and other key Games Delivery Partners, to ensure alignment and consistency in reporting and governance processes.
• Lead the identification and management of risks, issues, and opportunities related to governance and reporting, ensuring alignment with government standards and best practices.
• Establish and maintain strong, collaborative relationships with internal and external stakeholders to foster a culture of accountability, transparency, and continuous improvement.
• Support the development and implementation of policies, procedures, and tools that enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of governance and reporting activities.
• Contribute to the successful delivery of the Brisbane 2032 Games by ensuring all reporting and governance activities align with the overarching Games vision and objectives.
• Contribute to a workplace culture based on values and behaviours, ensuring a positive work environment that is safe, healthy, diverse, respectful and inclusive.

This role offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to one of the most significant events in Queensland's history, shaping the governance and reporting frameworks that will underpin the success of the Brisbane 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games.

Applications to remain current for 12 months.

About Us


Want to be part of a high-performing, award-winning team? Look no further than UniSuper. We’re recognised for our performance excellence as we strive to deliver exceptional retirements for our members, and our growth means opportunities to grow with us. Come be a part of our inclusive, passionate team and make a difference with UniSuper.
Over 70% of all funds under management (FUM) are managed in-house by our dedicated Investments team. We strongly believe our in-house management strategy ultimately leads to greater retirement outcomes for our members through lower fees, sustainable investment options and top quartile investment performance.
This is an opportunity to support the implementation of UniSuper’s Private Markets strategy by contributing to high‑quality investment analysis and long‑term value creation for members.
 

The role


As an Investment Analyst, Private Markets, you will support the origination, execution and ongoing management of direct and fund investments across the portfolio. You will work closely with the Private Markets team to deliver robust analysis, reporting and insights.

Develop and maintain financial models for direct private market investments
Assist with due diligence for acquisitions and divestments, including financial and valuation analysis
Support active asset and portfolio management, performance monitoring and reporting
Prepare investment recommendations and materials for internal committees and stakeholders
Generate and present investment ideas and research across relevant sectors
Collaborate with other asset class teams within Investments

About you


You are an experienced investment analyst with a strong foundation in private markets investing and financial analysis. You bring a proactive, detail‑oriented approach and communicate clearly with a broad range of stakeholders.

3–5 years’ experience in private markets, funds management, investment banking or professional services
Strong financial modelling capability and ability to synthesise commercial insights
Excellent written and verbal communication and presentation skills
Highly analytical with strong quantitative and technology skills
Self‑motivated, detail‑focused and able to work autonomously and in teams
Relevant tertiary and professional qualifications (e.g. CA or CFA)

What We Offer


UniSuper believes that the best way to achieve great things is when we come together and collaborate. Therefore, we ask you to be able to commit to 60% of your time in office. UniSuper is proud of our culture and benefits, which empower our people to achieve their full potential, thrive, and grow their career with us. These include:


• Development opportunities and learning support
• Flexible working and ways to flex
• Competitive total rewards and benefits
 

It should go without saying, but at UniSuper, we value and celebrate diversity and inclusion. We believe that a variety of perspectives, backgrounds, interests, abilities, and skills is crucial for delivering great retirement outcomes for our members. We invite you to apply for the roles that suit your career aspirations, even if you don’t meet all the requirements.

About the role
We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support the delivery of complex transformation and regulatory & compliance initiatives across our organisation. This role sits within the Transformation & Technology function and works closely with Product Owners, Delivery Leads and senior stakeholders to define, plan and deliver sustainable business outcomes.
  
The Senior Business Analyst will lead analysis activities across large and high-risk initiatives, often operating in environments with regulatory, compliance and operational complexity. This role requires strong planning capability, confidence engaging at executive level, and the ability to work effectively across both Agile and Waterfall delivery models.
  
Working as part of an inclusive and collaborative team your key responsibilities will include:
  

Lead end-to-end business analysis activities across complex initiatives
Operate effectively within Agile and Waterfall delivery environments
Plan and manage scope, dependencies, risks and deliverables
Engage and influence senior and executive stakeholders, including clear and concise written communication
Translate regulatory and compliance requirements into practical business and system solutions
Define and document business requirements, user stories and process models
Lead and coordinate testing activities, including UAT planning and execution
Identify and implement process improvement and automation opportunities
Provide coaching and mentoring support to other Business Analysts within the team
Contribute to continuous improvement of business analysis standards, tools and ways of working
  
  
What will you bring?

Significant experience as a Business Analyst delivering complex initiatives
Strong experience working across Agile and Waterfall methodologies
Demonstrated strength in planning, organisation and delivery management
Proven executive-level stakeholder management capability
Experience working within banking regulated and compliance-driven environments
Solid experience with testing and quality assurance activities
Experience delivering process improvement or automation initiatives
High standard of written and verbal communication
Experience with CRM platforms such as Dynamics or Salesforce
Exposure to UX/UI concepts, including wireframing is desirable
IIBA or equivalent business analysis certification is desirable


 
What we can offer you
There’s life at work, and life outside of work – and we understand that.
P&N Group is committed to creating a satisfying and fulfilling working environment, offering employee benefits and development opportunities that reward and help you perform to your potential and expand your experience and learning.

To help you live your best life through work, we offer an extensive range of employee benefits.

Work for a national multi-brand, customer-focused organisation
Enjoy flexible work arrangements
An incentive scheme for exceptional performance
Be supported with leadership and professional development
12 weeks paid parental leave for the primary care giver, and three weeks for the non-primary care giver
Generous discounts on home loans, personal loans, insurance and other banking products and services
Up to two weeks’ additional purchased leave per year
Two days’ paid volunteer leave each year to use with a community partner of your choice
An Employee Assistance Program
About us
P&N Group is amongst the largest customer-owned banking organisations in Australia, represented by P&N Bank in Western Australia and BCU Bank in New South Wales and southeast Queensland. Our purpose is to enrich the lives of our customers and their communities.
  
With over $9 billion in assets and a head office in Perth and corporate offices in Coffs Harbour and Brisbane, we service over 200,000 customers across Australia from all walks of life supported by over 600 employees in group services, retail banking, broker, and business banking roles.
  
We believe our culture is a real point of difference, and our values – Focus on Members, Improve & Learn and Trust & Collaborate – are fundamental to our ways of working. We offer a collaborative and inclusive workplace where our people feel a strong sense of belonging, are recognised, invested in, cared for, and are part of something bigger.
  
What does our recruitment process look like?
The process can include a phone screen, virtual and/or face-to-face interview, psychometric testing, reference checks and all relevant background checks.
At P&N Group, we want to leverage the numerous benefits and innovative gains that a diverse workforce brings, and especially the high engagement that comes from a culture built on belonging and inclusion. We encourage people of all abilities, backgrounds, identities, ages and cultures to apply.

If you need assistance or require flexibility to be able to fully participate in the application process, please email us at recruitment@pnbank.com.au

The closing date for applications is 01 May 2026
(Please note that candidate screening and interviews may be completed prior to advertisement closure date.)

Job Title: Investment Analyst - Real Estate Private Equity
Ad Text:
The Organisation
We have proudly partnered with a privately owned, entrepreneurial real estate group with a strong track record across Australia.

The business operates across a diversified portfolio including commercial office, retail, residential and land, with a clear focus on active asset management, repositioning and development-led value creation.

With significant capital being deployed and a growing pipeline of opportunities, the platform offers genuine exposure to the full real estate lifecycle in a fast-moving, high-performance environment.

The Role
This is a broad Investment Analyst role sitting within a lean, high-calibre team, offering exposure across transactions, asset management, leasing strategy and development.

You will work directly with senior leadership to support investment decision-making, while actively contributing to value creation initiatives across an evolving portfolio.

Key Responsibilities
Support acquisition and transaction activity including underwriting, due diligence and execution
Build and maintain financial models (DCF, feasibility and scenario analysis)
Assist with Investment Committee papers, submissions and deal documentation
Analyse asset performance, leasing metrics and income optimisation strategies
Support repositioning initiatives and capital deployment across existing assets
Assist in evaluating development and redevelopment opportunities
Undertake market research across multiple asset classes to identify trends and opportunities nationally
Work closely with internal teams and external advisors to support deal execution

About You
4–6 years’ experience in property, funds management, valuation, development or advisory
Strong financial modelling capability across DCF, feasibility or valuation
Exposure to transactions, development or asset management environments
Commercial mindset with the ability to think beyond the numbers
High attention to detail with strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to operate across multiple workstreams in a fast-paced environment

Why This Role
Broad exposure across acquisitions, development, asset management and leasing
Work directly with senior decision-makers in a lean, high-impact team
Opportunity to be involved in real repositioning and value creation initiatives
Diverse portfolio across multiple asset classes
Clear pathway to becoming a well-rounded investment professional

Should this position be of interest, please click ‘apply now’ or contact Theo Georgakopoulos for a confidential discussion on 03 9008 3908 or Theo@capstonerecruitment.com.au

JOB OVERVIEW:

We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support Biztrainer, a safety‑critical Learning and Contractor Management platform operating within a License to Operate landscape. This is a highly accountable role in a complex, integrated operational environment, where the quality of analysis and delivery directly impacts safety, compliance, and operational continuity.

This is not a standard BA role and requires strong operational engagement, sound judgement, and confidence working in high‑risk delivery contexts.


Key Responsibilities:

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis activities, including discovery, validation, and stakeholder playback
Facilitate and manage complex workshops with operational, safety, and technical stakeholders
Elicit, analyse, and document business, functional, and non‑functional requirements across integrated systems
Translate operational and compliance requirements into clear, actionable artefacts for delivery teams
Act as a trusted interface between business, operations, and technical teams
Leverage and adapt existing blueprints, patterns, and lessons learned from previous site implementations
Identify risks, gaps, and dependencies within tightly integrated operational workflows
Provide confident, constructive challenge where scope, assumptions, or delivery approaches may introduce operational risk

Scope & System Context

Biztrainer extends beyond a traditional LMS and includes:
Ongoing competency and learning compliance for operational site personnel
Contractor onboarding and contractor lifecycle management, a critical operational risk area
Deep integration with frontline operational workflows rather than back‑office processes
Interaction with safety‑critical and compliance‑heavy systems, including health and safety data

Required Experience & Capabilities

Senior‑level experience as a Business Analyst in complex enterprise environments
Strong stakeholder engagement capability, with proven credibility working directly with operational teams
Demonstrated experience running workshops end‑to‑end (discovery, validation, playback)
Ability to clearly translate between business needs and technical solutions
Experience in safety‑critical, operational, or compliance‑heavy industries (e.g. mining, oil & gas, heavy industry) highly regarded.
Solid understanding of integrated systems landscapes rather than standalone applications
Proactive, confident mindset with the ability to challenge constructively when required

Delivery Expectations

This role requires regular onsite presence (weekly or at a minimum monthly) to effectively engage with operational stakeholders
Not suitable for fully remote delivery
Operates in a high‑risk delivery environment with direct License to Operate implications, requiring maturity, decisiveness, and strong professional judgement


Please apply with current resume in Microsoft Word format only (.doc or .docx). Consulting Data Engineer

DyFlex Solutions is an established SAP Platinum Partner with offices across Australia and New Zealand. We are recognised as the local leader in SAP expertise, awarded many times as the best performing partner in multiple categories in the last few years. We deliver fit-for-purpose SAP solutions, with services delivering greenfield, upgrade and transformative implementations. We also deliver the project management, change management and ongoing support services to enable our staff to deliver excellence for our customers.

As the leader in SAP, Dyflex is now establishing a team to become the leader in enabling business centric AI & Data solutions. With SAP integrating Datasphere as an extension to Data Lakes such as Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery and Microsoft Fabric, we are seeking pragmatic thought leaders to help shape the shift of AI & Data engineering into the era of agentic/context engineering.

About the Role:
As a Consulting Data Engineer, you’ll design, build, and deploy scalable data pipeline and machine learning solutions that deliver real business value. You’ll use strong SQL and modern data stacks to create reliable, cost effective pipelines and support ML workloads. Experience with at least one of Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, MS Fabric is required, more than one a bonus. Experience with tools like Spark, dbt, Dataform is a plus but not required. You will work on meaningful technical challenges with autonomy and communicate technical outcomes clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

We value engineers who think creatively, communicate effectively, and engage confidently with stakeholders. We’re looking for engineers who do more than write code. You’ll listen to client challenges, dig into the core problem, help shape solutions, and explain them clearly. If you want to build something from the ground up with a team that’s already proven it can deliver meaningful outcomes, we’d like to hear from you.

Your tasks and responsibilities:

Build and maintain scalable data pipelines for ingesting, transforming, and delivering data
Manage and optimise databases, warehouses, and cloud storage solutions
Implement data quality frameworks and testing processes to ensure reliable systems
Design and deliver cloud-based solutions (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Take technical ownership of project components and lead small development teams
Engage directly with clients, translating business requirements into technical solutions
Champion best practices including version control, CI/CD, and infrastructure as code

Your qualifications and experience:

Hands-on data engineering experience in production environments for corporate, enterprise or public sector organisations
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL; experience with at least one additional language (e.g. Java, Typescript/Javascript)
Experience with modern frameworks such as Apache Spark, Airflow, dbt, Kafka, or Flink
Background in building ML pipelines, MLOps practices, or feature stores is highly valued
Proven expertise in relational databases, data modelling, and query optimisation
Experience with agentic coding tools such as Claude Code, Cortex in personal projects or commercial context, highly regarded
Demonstrated ability to solve complex technical problems independently
Excellent communication skills with ability to engage clients and stakeholders
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field

What we offer:

Work with SAP’s latest technologies on cloud as S/4HANA, BTP and Joule, plus Databricks, ML/AI tools and cloud platforms
A flexible and supportive work environment including work from home
Competitive remuneration and benefits including novated lease, birthday leave, salary packaging, wellbeing programme, additional purchased leave, and company-provided laptop
Comprehensive training budget and paid certifications (Databricks, SAP, cloud platforms, Snowflake, BigQuery)
Structured career advancement pathways with mentoring from senior engineers
Exposure to diverse industries and client environments

Join a renowned organisation delivering projects to some of Australia’s leading enterprises

DyFlex is committed to providing a safe, flexible and respectful environment for staff free from all forms of discrimination, bullying and harassment. We are proud of our diverse and inclusive team as only together we can continually improve ourselves and achieve best outcomes for our customers– we are the region's leading SAP Platinum Partner!

Please note that we can’t offer sponsorship for this role and we expect a clear statement about your legal rights in Australia, especially if you are applying from overseas.

Emesent is a world leader in autonomous drone technology, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Our solutions are transforming how mining, engineering, infrastructure, and public safety industries capture, understand, and act on spatial data. We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own and drive the next generation of our products.


As a Senior Product Manager, you will own the strategy and execution for one or more product lines within Emesent’s portfolio. You’ll work at the intersection of hardware, software, and autonomy defining what we build, why, and how we bring it to market. This is a high-autonomy role where you’ll set direction, make trade-offs, and drive outcomes. You’ll collaborate closely with engineering leads, commercial teams, and users to ensure our products deliver measurable value in complex, real-world environments.

What you’ll do

Product Strategy & Roadmap: Define the product strategy and roadmap for your area. Translate business objectives, market signals, and user needs into a clear, prioritised plan that balances near-term delivery with longer-term bets

Customer & Market Insight: Deeply understand our users workflows, pain points, and environments. Conduct field visits, user research, and competitive analysis to ensure product decisions are grounded in real-world evidence

Cross-Functional Leadership: Drive alignment across engineering, design, commercial, and client success teams. Drive sprint-level prioritisation within your squads and ensure the team is focused on the highest-impact work

Go-to-Market Execution: Partner with commercial and marketing teams to develop positioning, pricing, and launch strategies. Ensure users and internal teams are well- prepared for every release

Product Analytics: Define and monitor key product metrics. Use quantitative and qualitative data to inform decisions, measure outcomes, and continuously improve the user experience

Stakeholder Communication: Keep leadership and cross-functional stakeholders informed with clear, concise updates on progress, risks, and strategic decisions

What you’ll bring

5+ years of experience in product management, with demonstrated ownership of a product or product area end-to-end

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.

Proven ability to work effectively with engineering teams and translate technical complexity into clear product direction

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority across functions and seniority levels

Entrepreneurial mindset, you thrive in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and have a strong bias for action

Deep empathy for users and a passion for solving real-world problems in complex operating environments

Experience with Agile methodologies and tools such as Jira, Amplitude, or similar

Strongly regarded

Experience in the mining, geospatial, or survey industries

Track record of zero-to-one product development, taking a product from concept through to launch and market traction

Experience building or managing platform software products (APIs, SDKs, data pipelines, or developer-facing tools)

Experience with deep-tech or hardware-integrated products, where product decisions span physical and digital domains

The perks

9-day fortnight - every second Friday off

Employee Share Option Plan (ESOP) share in Emesent’s success

Work alongside a friendly and passionate team building cutting-edge technology

Genuine learning and development opportunities, including access to LinkedIn Learning

Employee Assistance Program

One full day of leave each year for your work anniversary

Novated Lease options

Who are we?

Emesent is a world leader in drone autonomy, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Founded in 2018 with deep roots in Australia’s globally recognised CSIRO, we’ve built a reputation for delivering high-quality spatial data capture across mining, infrastructure, survey, and construction industries. Our flagship product, Hovermap, is an autonomous mobile scanning platform that combines advanced collision avoidance and autonomous flight with LiDAR mapping to capture data in hazardous, GPS-denied, and hard-to-reach environments. Mountable on drones, vehicles, or carried as a backpack, Hovermap has set the standard for mobile mapping in underground mining and confined spaces. Our newest product, GX1, extends Emesent’s mapping technology into the AEC and survey markets. GX1 is a ground-based LiDAR scanner purpose-built for above-ground
environments delivering fast, accurate spatial capture for surveying and reality capture professionals.

With a rapidly growing global customer base and multiple product lines spanning hardware, desktop software, and cloud, Emesent is at an exciting inflection point  and we’re looking for people who want to help shape what comes next.


Why Emesent?
At Emesent, you’ll work on technology that is genuinely shaping the future of how industries capture and understand the physical world. We offer a dynamic, collaborative environment, competitive compensation, and real opportunities for professional growth. Join us in pushing the boundaries of robotics and spatial intelligence.

Job Title: Senior Vice President Finance - Group Functions & Process Excellence
Ad Text:
About Toll Group

At Toll, we do more than just logistics - we move the businesses that move the world. Our 16,000 team members can help solve any logistics, transport, or supply chain challenge – big or small. We have been supporting our customers for more than 130 years. Today, we support more than 20,000 customers worldwide with 500 sites in 27 markets, and a forwarding network spanning 150 countries. We are proudly part of Japan Post — www.tollgroup.com

The Senior Vice President Finance - Group Functions & Process Excellence is a strategic leadership role within the Finance Division, reporting directly to the Group CFO. This is a hybrid role, combining two critical mandates that together drive cost discipline and operational excellence across the organization: 

Finance Business Partner to Group Functions – specifically HR, IT, Legal & Compliance, and Enterprise Services – providing financial stewardship, cost management, and strategic insight to enable these functions to operate efficiently and within budget on a global scale.

Ownership of Finance processes globally, driving continuous improvement, standardization, and optimization to enhance the efficiency, accuracy, and effectiveness of Finance operations across all markets.

The SVP is expected to bring exceptional financial acumen, strong commercial acumen, deep analytics capabilities, and deep process expertise, coupled with the leadership gravitas to engage and influence senior stakeholders at the Group Function level and across geographies.

Role Responsibilities include:


Finance Business Partnering

Serve as the primary Finance partner to the global heads of group functions, providing proactive financial advice, cost analysis, and performance insights.

Lead annual budgeting, quarterly forecasting, monthly business review, and long-range financial planning for the four Group Functions, ensuring alignment with Group-wide financial targets.

Monitor, analyse, and challenge cost performance across Group Functions, identifying opportunities to improve cost efficiency without compromising operational effectiveness.

Provide clear and timely financial reporting to Group Function leaders and the Group CFO, translating complex financial data into actionable insights.

Partner with Group Function leaders on business cases, investment decisions, and resource allocation, ensuring robust financial evaluation and governance.

Drive accountability for cost management within Group Functions, establishing and maintaining appropriate financial controls and governance frameworks.

Support Group Function leaders in managing third-party vendor and contract costs, ensuring value for money and alignment with approved budgets.

Finance Process Ownership & Optimisation

Own and govern the end-to-end Finance processes globally, including but not limited to procure-to-pay, record-to-report, and financial planning & analysis processes.

Develop and implement a global Finance process optimisation roadmap, identifying inefficiencies and designing solutions that improve speed, accuracy, and scalability.

Establish and maintain global process standards, policies, and operating procedures, ensuring consistency and compliance across all markets.

Lead process transformation initiatives, leveraging technology, automation, and best practices to modernise Finance operations.

Work closely with the Finance technology and systems teams to ensure process design is effectively supported by Finance systems and tools.

Measure and track process performance through defined KPIs, driving continuous improvement and accountability across the Finance function.

Act as a centre of expertise on Finance process matters, providing guidance and support to regional and local Finance teams globally.

People Leadership

Lead, inspire, and develop a high-performing team of approximately 20 Finance professionals across business partnering and process ownership disciplines.

Set clear direction and performance expectations, foster a culture of accountability and continuous learning, and build strong succession pipelines.

Manage team resources effectively across both workstreams, and champion diversity, equity, and inclusion within the team.

What you bring:


Exceptional financial acumen with strong skills in planning, budgeting, forecasting, and cost management, underpinned by deep knowledge of financial controls, governance frameworks, and the ability to translate complex data into compelling insights for senior audiences.

Proven expertise in Finance process design, re-engineering, and optimization, with hands-on experience in ERP platforms (e.g. SAP), Finance automation tools, and leading cross-functional transformation programs.

Exceptional stakeholder management and influencing skills, with the gravitas to build trusted relationships and drive alignment across functions, cultures, and geographies without direct authority.

Strategic yet detail-oriented, with a strong analytical mindset, continuous improvement orientation, and the resilience to lead effectively through complexity, ambiguity, and change.

Results-driven, inclusive leader of high integrity — collaborative, globally minded, and deeply committed to building high-performing teams and maintaining the highest standards of financial governance.

Proven people leadership capability, with experience building, developing, and inspiring sizeable teams, fostering a culture of accountability, continuous learning, and inclusion across diverse and geographically dispersed groups.

Qualifications:


Bachelor or Master degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business Administration, or a related discipline

What moves you?
 

At Toll, you can help play a vital role in delivering what matters. From food, fuel, medicine and rescue services, we keep businesses and communities thriving. Every day brings change. We see that as an opportunity. To be curious. To ask the right questions. And build meaningful connections. Because finding new ways to solve problems is what we do. With a bold vision to expand our global reach, our 16,000+ people bring a passion for progress. We collaborate in friendly, caring teams, supported by approachable leaders who give us the autonomy to quickly make decisions with impact. Learn and grow with industry-leading training, alongside talented experts. Feel empowered to take on diverse challenges and new responsibilities to move you, our customers, and our world further.

Are you excited about this role but are concerned you don’t meet all the requirements? If you have similar skills and are willing to learn then we encourage you to apply anyway. We know that some people hesitate to apply for jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Toll, we value a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re interested in this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly then please talk to us – you may be just the right candidate for this or other roles we have coming up.

At Toll everyone is welcome including those of all ages, ethnicities, genders and abilities. 

To find out more about us visit www.careers.tollgroup.com

You must be entitled to work in Australia and be prepared to undertake pre-employment checks including a criminal history check and medical.

Principal Consulting AI / Data Engineer

DyFlex Solutions is an established SAP Platinum Partner with offices across Australia and New Zealand. We are recognised as the local leader in SAP expertise, awarded many times as the best performing partner in multiple categories in the last few years. We deliver fit-for-purpose SAP solutions, with services delivering greenfield, upgrade and transformative implementations. We also deliver the project management, change management and ongoing support services to enable our staff to deliver excellence for our customers.

As the leader in SAP, Dyflex is now establishing a team to become the leader in enabling business centric AI & Data solutions. With SAP integrating Datasphere as an extension to Data Lakes such as Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery and Microsoft Fabric, we are seeking pragmatic thought leaders to help shape the shift of AI & Data engineering into the era of agentic/context engineering.

About the Role:
As a Principal Consulting AI / Data Engineer, you will design, build, and optimise enterprise-scale data and AI solutions, while also leading engagements with executive stakeholders. This role requires both technical depth and consulting acumen: you will be expected to explain complex technical concepts clearly to non-technical directors, C-suite executives, and senior customer stakeholders. You’ll play a critical role in shaping DyFlex’s growing Data and AI practice, delivering innovative, business-aligned solutions that drive measurable outcomes.

Your tasks and responsibilities:

Design, build, and maintain scalable data and AI solutions using Databricks, cloud platforms, and modern frameworks
Lead solution architecture discussions with clients, ensuring alignment of technical delivery with business strategy
Present to and influence executive-level stakeholders, including boards, C-suite, and senior directors
Translate highly technical solutions into clear business value propositions for non-technical audiences
Mentor and guide teams of engineers and consultants to deliver high-quality solutions
Champion best practices across data engineering, MLOps, and cloud delivery
Build DyFlex’s reputation as a trusted partner in Data & AI through thought leadership and client advocacy

Your qualifications and experience:

Proven expertise in delivering enterprise-grade data engineering and AI solutions in production environments for corporate, enterprise or public sector organisations
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL, plus experience with Spark, Airflow, dbt, Kafka, or Flink
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and AI/Data platforms including Databricks, Snowflake, Google BigQuery, Microsoft Fabric
Ability to confidently communicate and present at C-suite level, simplifying technical concepts into business impact
Track record of engaging senior executives and influencing strategic decisions
Strong consulting and stakeholder management skills with client-facing experience
Experience with agentic coding tools such as Claude Code, Cortex in personal projects or commercial context, highly regarded
Background in MLOps, ML pipelines, or AI solution delivery highly regarded
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field

What we offer:

Work with SAP’s latest technologies on cloud as S/4HANA, BTP and Joule, plus Databricks, ML/AI tools and cloud platforms
A flexible and supportive work environment including work from home
Competitive remuneration and benefits including novated lease, birthday leave, salary packaging, wellbeing programme, additional purchased leave, and company-provided laptop
Comprehensive training budget and paid certifications (Databricks, SAP, cloud platforms)
Structured career advancement pathways with opportunities to lead large-scale client programs
Exposure to diverse industries and client environments, including executive-level engagement
Join a renowned organisation delivering projects to some of Australia’s leading enterprises

DyFlex is committed to providing a safe, flexible and respectful environment for staff free from all forms of discrimination, bullying and harassment. We are proud of our diverse and inclusive team as only together we can continually improve ourselves and achieve best outcomes for our customers– we are the region's leading SAP Platinum Partner!

Please note that we can’t offer sponsorship for this role and we expect a clear statement about your legal rights in Australia, especially if you are applying from overseas.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, home and digital solutions.

Optus.ai is our centralised AI, Analytics, Business Intelligence and Data Science function. Our purpose is to accelerate the transformation of Optus with AI. We design, build and operate AI-enabled products, agents and copilots that augment decisions, automate processes and improve experiences across the enterprise.

We're looking for an AI Product Manager to support the development and delivery of AI products within our Customer, Enabling, or Technology product portfolios.

What you'll do:

Reporting to the Senior Director of AI Product, you'll contribute to the end-to-end product lifecycle for a single product or squad, working within a defined product strategy and be accountable for:

Supporting product strategy execution and roadmap delivery for a single AI product or squad, aligned to Optus.ai objectives and business goals.
Contributing to product backlogs by writing clear user stories and acceptance criteria that translate business requirements into actionable development work.
Conducting user research initiatives including interviews, surveys, usability testing, and behavioural analysis to understand user needs and validate solutions.
Collaborating with cross-functional squads including engineering, data science, design, and business stakeholders through the product development lifecycle.
Defining and analysing key product metrics and KPIs to track product health, usage, and business impact, using insights to inform product decisions.
Participating in agile product development practices, including sprint planning, backlog refinement, and retrospectives to ensure high-quality delivery.
Building and maintaining stakeholder relationships, gathering feedback from diverse groups to inform product decisions and maintain alignment.

What you'll bring:

We're looking for someone who's excited by the challenge of building AI products that deliver real value and eager to grow their product craft.

Must-haves:

2-4 years of experience in product management roles within technology companies.
Demonstrated experience supporting product development from concept to launch.
Experience working with cross-functional teams and contributing to product backlogs, roadmaps, and success metrics.
Strong commitment to user-centred design with ability to balance user needs, business objectives, and technical constraints.
Proficiency in product analytics and using data insights to drive product decisions.
Understanding of AI/ML concepts and ability to work effectively with technical teams.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with ability to articulate product concepts clearly and build stakeholder relationships.
Analytical problem-solving skills with structured approach to breaking down challenges into actionable solutions.

Nice-to-haves:

Experience working with AI/ML products or platforms.
Experience in telecommunications or highly regulated industries.

The good stuff…

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.
Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.
Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.
Free Optus bus from Macquarie University Metro Station (every 6-8 minutes); morning and afternoon.

We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170. We're here to help. When you contact us, please advise your preferred method of contact, and we will be in touch within 48 hours.

Job Reference: 19677

Why You Will Be Excited About This Role

At Healius, we all have one thing in common: we are passionate about making a difference to the health of Australians. And right now, there's never been a more exciting time to join us. As we continue our transformation into a stronger, more agile organisation, we are focused on delivering excellent customer and patient experiences, smarter ways of working, and providing a great place to work.

This is a pivotal and highly visible role supporting the Head of Group FP&A and Investor Relations, through high‑quality budgeting, forecasting, reporting and analysis. You'll work closely with senior leaders and operational stakeholders to support the integrity of financial systems and contribute to key deliverables such as long‑term financial planning, monthly and annual reporting, and performance monitoring across the business.

About Us

Healius has an expansive network of over 80 medical laboratories and 2,000 patient collection centres operating across metropolitan, regional and remote Australia.

We provide 1 in every 4 pathology services in Australia, extending from exclusively servicing some of Australia's largest and most complex private and public hospitals, to small Indigenous Australian communities.

This means that you will be working in a business large enough to offer job stability and security, interstate mobility, and opportunities to fulfil your career ambitions.

What You Will Be Doing

As the Senior FP&A Analyst, you will provide financial planning, reporting and analytical support. Partnering closely with the Commercial Finance teams, senior leaders and operational stakeholders, to support sound financial governance and performance outcomes.

Key responsibilities include:

Assist with preparation of the annual budgets and monthly rolling forecasts across the Group and supporting presentations to be distributed to the Executive Leadership Team
Business modelling (including 3-way-models), report development and analysis for profit improvement initiatives
Cash flow forecasting and working with Treasury as required
Assist with design & implementation of standardised models for the functional finance teams across both legacy & contemporary systems where applicable
Analysis of key revenue & cost drivers as part of monthly Board reporting and other ad-hoc analysis deliverables
Preparation of biannual analysis packs and external reporting documents such as the investor presentations for Half Year and Year End reporting
Improving existing financial reporting & analytical capabilities, including analysis of macro and micro economic factors which impact various areas of the business
Collaborate with broader business functions and other teams within Finance such as Financial Reporting & Controls, Commercial Finance, Accounts Receivable and Procurement

What Type of Person Will Be Successful

To be successful in this role, you will be an experienced accounting or finance professional with a strong background in financial modelling, management accounting, budgeting and forecasting and corporate reporting. You will bring strong analytical and numerical skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to manage competing priorities while delivering accurate and timely financial outcomes.

You will ideally bring:

Strong foundation in accounting and financial analysis, such as trend analysis ability to derive insights for decision support
Financial modelling experience including preparation of 3-way model and cash flow forecasting, prior experience in Deals / Transactions / M&A a plus
Advanced Excel skills; experience with Power BI, SAP, or Anaplan highly desirable.
High attention to detail with a commitment to accuracy.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to present financial information clearly.
A proactive, solutions-focused mindset and desire to drive process improvement.
Strong organisational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities independently.
A collaborative, team-first approach to work.
Integrity, professionalism, and a high standard of ethical conduct. Demonstrated ability to deal with confidential and sensitive information in a professional manner
A bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
CA/CPA qualification preferred
3-5 years' experience in a financial analysis or similar finance role.

We believe everyone leads, no matter what role they are in. Our ways of leading are the behaviours that we expect our people to embody and are: Customer Focused, Accountable, Collaborative, Credible & Resourceful.

.

Benefits, Perks and Wellbeing

As part of working for Healius, you will have access to a range of benefits & perks including:

Benefits platform - Reduce the impact of the cost of living with everyday savings with 500+ of Australia's most-loved brands, (from the weekly grocery shop to premium perks like holidays).
Fitness Passport - Access top fitness centres & pools across Australia at a subsidised rate.
Corporate health insurance discounts, banking benefits and novated leasing salary packaging.
Career growth and development opportunities with access to a comprehensive library of online courses and learning paths via our e-learning platform.
Confidential health and well-being support through our Employee Assistance Program, available to all employees and their family members.

Our Commitment to Diversity And Inclusion

Healius is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace. We seek to recruit from diverse applicants and create teams with skills, perspectives and experiences that complement each other at work. We welcome suitably qualified applicants regardless of age, ethnicity, socio-cultural background, disability, sexual orientation or gender identity.

Healius is a part of the Commonwealth Government's Veteran Employment Committment (VEC) and has been recognised as a Veteran Employment Supporter. Veterans and candidates with Australian Defence Force (ADF) experience are encouraged to apply.

How To Apply

If you would like further details please email careers@healius.com.au.

Senior Policy Analyst (ECEC)

Are you an organised policy professional? AERO is seeking a talented and driven Senior Policy Analyst with a background in ECEC to help contribute evidence-based policy advice on a range of complex educational issues.

Offering a competitive base salary starting at $116,222 (commensurate with skills and experience) plus 12% superannuation 
Full time temporary contract until 31 January 2027
Flexible location, work from home (Australia-wide)   

About The Organisation 

The Australian Education Research Organisation (AERO) is Australia’s national education evidence body. AERO is working towards excellent and equitable outcomes for all children and young people by generating and making high-quality evidence accessible and enhancing the use of evidence in Australian education.  

AERO is bringing together people who are passionate about evidence-informed policy and practice for schools and early childhood services. Our team is collegial, creative and committed to fulfilling AERO’s potential to make an impact. We value agile thinkers who can help shape AERO as a new, dynamic organisation, and team players who enjoy working across projects and in diverse, interdisciplinary teams. Many of our people have ongoing connections to research, policy and practice organisations, helping us to bridge the gap between evidence generation and translation. 

For more information about AERO, please see https://edresearch.edu.au 

Purpose of the role 

The role undertakes complex policy analysis / research to support policymakers and key stakeholders in using evidence effectively. Serving as the conduit between policymakers, stakeholders, and researchers, this role ensures high-impact findings are accessible, understandable, and easy to enact in a policy context.

Through rigorous analysis and strategic collaboration, the position drives informed decision-making and system improvement within the early childhood and broader education sector, either through direct partnerships or policy influence mechanisms.

Accountabilities: 

Conduct advanced policy research and analysis, including investigating and critically analysing issues and opportunities relating to early childhood / education.
Develop and implement strategies to maximise the influence of AERO’s research on system programs and policies.
Provide high quality policy advice on a wide variety of issues in education, including ECEC services, schools, child and student outcomes, and evidence use in practice.
Prepare, convene and facilitate workshops for stakeholders, and implement effective follow-up to maximise stakeholder engagement and dissemination of AERO’s work.
Prepare advice and recommendations to internal and external stakeholders that conveys complex concepts and information on early childhood / education topics in an appropriate format and manner.
Advise, support and collaborate with other AERO teams as required, with a particular focus on ensuring effective incorporation of AERO’s strategic policy agenda in their work.

Knowledge, Skills and Experience 

Essential requirements: 

Tertiary qualifications and/or relevant experience in policy (preferably public policy), public administration and/or early childhood/ education.

Knowledge and experience:

Excellent understanding of policy landscapes and policy frameworks and governance.
Understanding of ECEC (ideally) or education landscape and workings in Australia.

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That’s why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members.

We are dedicated to building a high‑performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.

Your New Role

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Governance within the Technology, Digital and Data domain, the Change Analyst plays a key role in enabling AustralianSuper’s strategic objectives, including enabling change arising from governance, risk and regulatory activities.

You will provide hands‑on change management expertise across complex initiatives, supporting the Fund as it responds to increased regulatory pressures, evolving member expectations and rapid technological change. You will be responsible for developing and delivering structured change approaches, undertaking impact and readiness assessments, and supporting effective adoption through clear communication, learning and change measurement activities.

In this role, you’ll:

Deliver structured change management approaches to support strategic, governance, risk and regulatory initiatives within the Technology, Digital and Data domain
Conduct change impact and readiness assessments, including impacts to governance arrangements, policies, standards and controls
Define current, transition and future states through analysis, desktop reviews and stakeholder workshops
Develop and deliver core change artefacts including stakeholder maps, sponsor roadmaps, communication and training plans
Identify and manage change risks and anticipated resistance, supporting mitigation and resistance management strategies
Track and report on adoption, utilisation and proficiency metrics to embed sustainable change outcomes

What You’ll Need

Tertiary qualifications in business, human resources or a related discipline (essential), with post‑graduate or agile/project management qualifications desirable
3–5 years’ experience in a Change Analyst or similar role within complex or fast‑paced environments
Demonstrated experience applying structured change management principles, methodologies and tools
A strong understanding of how people experience and transition through change
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with resilience and adaptability to work through ambiguity

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, supporting our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we’re happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What’s Next

Apply now if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes, and would like the opportunity to help shape how AustralianSuper enables sustainable, well‑governed change.

Important Information

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. Summary

As Strategic Program Pipeline Lead you will own the end‑to‑end framework that turns ideas into measurable outcomes - governing how strategic initiatives are prioritised, delivered and realised across the organisation.

You’ll bring clarity and confidence to decision‑making by providing independent assurance, transparent reporting and trusted insights on strategic programs and cost‑reduction initiatives. As the business owner of the tools, systems and datasets that underpin the strategic pipeline, you’ll set the standard for how value is tracked and realised.

Beyond governance, you’ll lead continuous improvement and build financial awareness enabling teams to work consistently and effectively.

The Organisation

Airservices Australia is a government-owned organisation responsible for the safe and efficient management of 11% of the world’s airspace and provision of aviation rescue fire fighting services at Australia’s busiest airports.

Our people are our greatest asset with a dynamic and diverse team operating from locations across the country - from bustling cities to regional and remote locations, including an island. This team keep are helping to secure the future of Australia’s skies and keep it amongst the safest in the world.

We connect people with their world safely – linking family and friends, generating economic activity, creating jobs, and facilitating trade and tourism.

Airservices is committed to fostering a culture that is diverse, inclusive, and respectful.  We encourage motivated individuals who love what they do, value a service first mindset and embrace a challenge to explore a career with Airservices. In return you will be a valued team member, be offered flexibility and experience a meaningful career in an exciting, ever-evolving aviation industry.

Our Leaders

Leaders at Airservices demonstrate our Leadership Standard in the way they Know, Focus and Value our people every day. Our leaders role model our values of Safe always, Service first, Love what we do, Work as one, Own it to connect people with their world safely.

An Airservices leader leads inclusively, thinks strategically, collaborates and communicates with impact to deliver outcomes that support our culture of trust, care and accountability.

The Role & The Team

By owning the framework that converts strategic ideas into measurable outcomes you can be instrumental in governing how initiatives are prioritised, delivered and realised across the organisation. You will provide independent assurance, clear reporting and trusted insights on strategic programs and cost‑reduction initiatives, while acting as business owner for the tools, systems and data that enable value realisation.

Please see the link below to view the full Position Description:

 PD Final Strategic Program Pipeline Lead.docx

The Person

You are a credible leader who brings rigour, insight and independence to complex strategic environments. Known for your analytical strength and ability to cut through ambiguity, you operate as a trusted advisor to senior leaders—balancing assurance with pragmatism, and insight with action.

You will partner closely with:

Executive Leadership Team and the CEO on strategy delivery, assurance and decision‑making

Senior Leadership Team to drive consistency, value realisation and delivery discipline

Portfolio Leads to govern, prioritise and track strategic initiatives

Finance Division Leadership to strengthen financial insight, controls and performance outcomes

To be successful in this role, you bring:

Formal qualifications in Portfolio, Program and/or Project Management, and/ or a tertiary or recognised qualification in commercial or business related field supported by extensive practical experience
Around 15 years’ experience and business acumen across Portfolio, Program and Project Management, including
10+ years in leadership roles
At least 5 years operating at Senior Leadership level
Demonstrated success in defining, assessing and uplifting project and delivery capability in complex organisations
Strong conceptual and analytical skills, with the ability to translate data into clear, objective insights
A proven ability to develop innovative solutions, shape thinking and influence outcomes at scale
Highly developed relationship‑building capability, enabling effective partnerships across diverse stakeholders
Experience across both public and private sectors (desirable), bringing breadth of perspective and adaptability
Experience in the aviation sector (desirable)

At Airservices Australia, we embrace and thrive on diversity of thought, perspectives, and capabilities because we know the benefits of having a workforce that is representative of the community that we serve. We appreciate everyone is unique, and acknowledge diversity in thought leads to greater transparency and improved consideration in our decision making.

We believe a diverse workforce supported by an inclusive culture is central to our success. We actively encourage and welcome candidates from all cultural backgrounds, gender identities, people living with disability and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples to apply.

Job Title: Senior Analyst - Credit, Market and Liquidity Risk
Ad Text:
Company Description

Empower Australia’s Payments Future with Cuscal  

At Cuscal, your skills drive change and make a real impact. Whether you’re supporting our clients’ customers in our Fraud Operations Contact Centre or delivering innovative solutions in Product Delivery, your contribution helps shape the payments industry’s future.  

Join a team that values collaboration, innovation and excellence. With a focus on professional growth and flexibility, Cuscal is where you can thrive.  

Forward with Cuscal.  

Job Description

We are looking for a Senior Analyst, Credit Risk to join our Financial Risk team on a 12-month maximum term contract, to support in the strategic reporting of Financial Risks.

The Senior Analyst will support the Senior Manager Market Risk and Head of Financial Risk in the strategic reporting of Financial Risks to internal stakeholders, ALCO, and Board. The Senior Analyst will develop and maintain business requirements documentation for all reports and implement/develop analytical solutions and apply Machine Learning and AI for deep insights

You’ll also make an impact by: 

Risk Data & Reporting 

This role has responsibility for establishing and maintaining effective management practices over risk data, allocated systems and reports, including:
Effectively and efficiently enable Risk to meet statutory and regulatory reporting obligations and produce management reports to support data driven decisions
Lead the development and implementation of a suite of financial models, dashboards and reports to uplift Risk capabilities
Establish and maintain standardised data hierarchies to facilitate consistent analysis linking risk results to internal/external drivers
Maintain a robust control environment over Cuscal’s financial risk data
Support the Head of Financial Risk and the Senior Manager Market Risk in maintaining Policies and Procedures for the collection, storage, structure, analysis and sharing of financial data
Automate processes end-to-end and facilitate robust and reliable insight and analysis

Stakeholder Management & Collaboration 

As the systems support several stakeholders and company initiatives, embedding effective stakeholder management and collaboration practices within the Data and Strategic Initiatives team is a key part of this role
Test the accuracy of the newly designed and implemented solutions.
Monitor the accuracy of the required output.
Work closely with the business for continuous improvement opportunities.
Utilise data analytics to provide actionable insights and recommendations to enhance risk management practices.
Produce quality, concise and accurate risk and compliance reports using risk data and insights to management, committees, and the Board.
Collaborate with business leaders and stakeholders to foster a data-driven culture.
Oversee and deliver key data governance projects on time and within scope.
Work closely with data analyst and reporting teams to ensure data is readily available to produce accurate and timely delivery of reports.

Other accountabilities

Perform scenario/stress tests to inform risk settings and assist in risk model validation activities.
Deep experience in regulatory reporting and compliance related to treasury risk. Remediation and transformation uplift requiring:
Treasury System knowledge (e.g. Murex, Calypso etc)
Project management skills (problem solving, requirement analysis, business process improvement)

What you’ll bring 

You’ve got the ability to think critically and to solve complex problems. You’ve got excellent communication and an eye for detail. You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are keen to contribute to a dynamic team.

To succeed in this role, you’ll have: 

Minimum 2 years of experience in analytics, insights and reporting within the finance and banking industry.
Demonstrated experience in risk management, risk management software, risk reporting, analytics or related field.
Expert skills in data analysis tools, data visualisation and relevant software (e.g. Excel, SQL, Power BI, Tableau).
Ability to build relationships, engage and influence senior management and key stakeholders on risk-related matters, communicating complex topics in simple, understandable terms.
Knowledge of regulatory requirements and industry standards related to risk reporting.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to build effective workplace relationships.
Ability to work with a high degree of autonomy and be able to demonstrate sound judgment.
Proficient in executing change management activities.
Additional Information

Why Cuscal? 

At Cuscal, you’ll find a strong, successful company that’s reimagining the future. And our team is right there at the heart of it all.   

Here, you’ll deliver or support interesting, ground-breaking work that has real impact - on Australia’s financial services sector and the millions of customers it serves.  

You’ll innovate alongside skilled, smart, connected teams. 

And you’ll build an impressive, fulfilling career that continues to grow.   

You’ll also enjoy a range of benefits, including: 

Recognition and growth: Celebrate achievements through IGNITE and grow with tailored development opportunities. 
Wellbeing focus: Access initiatives supporting physical, mental, and financial health, plus discounts via ‘Cuscal Advantage.’ 
Diversity and inclusion: Join a workplace that values different perspectives and flexible work arrangements. 

Join us and reimagine the future 

Ready to make an impact? Click APPLY to join today. 

If you’re excited about this opportunity, we’d love to explore how you can contribute to our vision for the future. Screening and interviews may occur before the job ad closing date, so don’t wait - apply now. 

Cuscal is an equal opportunity employer committed to an equitable, diverse, and socially inclusive work environment and a positive, barrier-free recruitment process.  

We welcome applicants from an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander heritage, people living with a disability, LGBTQIA+ and people from culturally diverse backgrounds to explore career opportunities with Cuscal.

At Wesfarmers Health we strive to make health, beauty and wellness experiences simpler, more affordable and easier to access for all Australians.

Our portfolio includes well-known names like Priceline, as well as our MediAesthetics brands - Clear Skincare Clinics and SILK Laser Clinics. In the digital space, we're proud to have SISU Health and InstantScripts—and most recently, we've launched our newest retail beauty stores, atomica.





About us:

What began in 1910 as a small co-operative of just three pharmacies has grown into Wesfarmers Health—a leading Australian organisation in health, beauty, and wellness.

Today, Wesfarmers Health proudly employs over 3,600 team members, all committed to delivering accessible, innovative, and trusted health and beauty services to Australians nationwide, across retail, medi-aesthetics, digital health, and wholesale and supply chain operations.

What you'll do:

We are seeking a Commercial Analyst - Pharmacy & Beauty Retail to join our Commercial Finance team. Reporting to the Senior Commercial Manager - Pharmacy & Beauty, you'll play a key role in powering performance across our Priceline Pharmacy franchise network and our company‑owned Beauty retail businesses.

You will turn financial and operational data into clear, actionable insights that help leaders understand performance, identify opportunities and make smarter commercial decisions. This is a hands‑on role with exposure across a dynamic and fast‑growing retail environment, including emerging business units like InstantScripts and atomica.

Key Activities Include:

Deliver weekly, month‑end and planning reports with clear insights and commentary
Analyse financial and operational performance to identify risks, opportunities and drivers of sales, margin and cost outcomes
Support the Store Operations leadership team with commercial reporting, business cases and commercial recommendations
Develop financial models, scenario analysis and forecasts across Pharmacy and Beauty Retail
Improve visibility, control and forecasting of above‑store costs
Partner across operational, merchandise, trade and strategic teams in a matrix structure

Overview

We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support a large-scale, multi-year transformation program within a complex, regulated environment. Company description:



Zurich is a leading multi-line insurer that serves its customers in global and local markets. With about 55,000 employees, it provides a wide range of property and casualty, life insurance products and services in more than 215 countries and territories. Zurich's customers include individuals, small businesses, and mid-sized and large companies, as well as multinational corporations.



Job description:



A new transformational adventure awaits you…

How does 5 weeks of annual leave, a fulfilling career you'll genuinely enjoy, and working for a reputable organization that promotes wellbeing and drives digital transformation sound to you?

As one of Forbes 2024 ‘World's Best Employers' and Fortune's ‘World's Most Admired Companies' for 2025, Zurich - a leader in the insurance industry, is on a journey towards a brighter future… A brighter future for you - focused on your career, your wellbeing, and your community - as well as our customers and the planet.

Join us on this thrilling journey as we work towards our vision of becoming Australia's leading digital insurer. If you're eager to be part of a values-driven global organisation that's experiencing real growth and transformation, and if you share our commitment to making a tangible difference and taking continuous steps towards a sustainable future for our people, customers, communities, and our planet, then keep reading!


Let's create a brighter future together, let's make a difference

It's important to know, what you do matters. At Zurich, we don't just cover, we care.

This is a pivotal new role within the Claims Planning & Performance function, created to uplift, mature, and embed disciplined workforce planning capability within one of Australia's leading multiline insurance businesses.

As Workforce Planning Leader, you will be responsible for designing and coordinating long‑term strategic workforce plans and mid‑to‑short‑term capacity plans to ensure the Claims business is equipped with the right resources, at the right time, with the right skills.

You will work closely with Claims Crews across a range of products (General Insurance, Life Insurance, Travel Insurance), Planning & Performance Analysts, Operational Excellence Leads, Finance, Actuarial, Underwriters, and frontline leaders to create a unified, data-driven approach to forecasting demand and planning capacity.

While initially focused on Travel Claims, this role is positioned to grow into an enterprise workforce planning capability, expanding into additional claims lines such as Motor, Retail Life, and Property as our planning maturity evolves.

You will also be responsible for the following:

Leading the development and refinement of long term strategic workforce plans across Claims.
Building and maintaining robust capacity plans that support operational stability and future growth.
Designing data rich planning models that guide hiring, training, and seasonal workforce decisions.
Partnering with Claims leaders to translate business initiatives into measurable workforce impacts.
Providing clear workforce insights, risk assessments, and planning recommendations to senior leaders.
Driving the uplift of planning discipline and consistency across Claims Crews.

Important to your success - let's grow together

You're someone who thrives in a collaborative, fast moving environment and enjoys bringing clarity to complex operational challenges. You work with curiosity, influence with confidence, and build strong relationships by providing insights leaders can trust.

In order to be successful, you will have the following:

Experience in forecasting, capacity planning, or workforce analytics in a contact centre or operational environment.
Strong analytical capability, with proficiency in planning tools or modelling techniques.
Experience with analytical and data tools, such as SQL, Power Platform, Python, Excel.
A solid understanding of operational metrics such as AHT, shrinkage, productivity, and service levels.
Demonstrated ability to partner with leaders and influence decision making through data led insights.
Experience building, maintaining, or improving forecasting models or planning tools.
Strong communication skills and the ability to translate technical detail into clear, practical guidance

Belong. Zurich is here to support you

There are so many reasons why Zurich is a great place to be and the right choice for you, but here are just a few.

Enjoy 5 weeks of annual leave for permanent staff through our Z leave initiative, along with 15 days of personal/carer's leave as we empower our people to manage their own wellbeing. Plus, access to Family Care (parental leave).
Benefit from a hybrid working arrangement - 3 days in the office and 2 days working from home per week for full-time employees.
Access programs to stay healthy and feel healthier, receive an AUD$500 wellbeing payment per year, and a AUD$295 new starter e-voucher for your work-from-home setup (flex work - hybrid working), along with other great benefits through our Shine portal.
Grow and thrive in your career with our award-winning training programs. We support and encourage your development because our strength lies in our people, and we value your individuality.
Work with global, diverse, and inclusive teams; explore your passions, fuel innovation, drive equity, collaborate, and make a positive difference beyond your role.
Take advantage of global mobility opportunities across more than 200 countries worldwide.
Volunteer and work in our state-of-the-art Green Star rated offices (when you are in the office) and dress for your day.
We plant a tree for every new employee.
Not to mention our various employer of choice awards/memberships - WGEA; Family Inclusive Workplace; Gold AWEI Employer, Pride in Diversity… to name a few.

We could go on, but the main point is that Zurich is a great place to be, where you can truly belong, be yourself, maintain work/life balance, and thrive in a supportive environment.

Zurich is an equal opportunity employer.  We are committed to ensuring that our recruitment process is fair and accessible for all candidates. If you require any special accommodations to participate in our recruitment process, we encourage you to please let us know at the time of your application.

So, if you want to come on an adventure that will build a brighter future together, and feel a sense of togetherness, then please apply today to start your adventure!

The Company

This is a unique opportunity to join a high-growth, globally recognised organisation that has disrupted its industry through innovation, science-led thinking, and a commitment to sustainability. With a strong presence across international markets, the business continues to evolve, driven by a clear vision and a focus on delivering high-quality products that make a positive impact.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Head of FP&A, this role sits at the heart of the Group Finance function and plays a critical role in supporting enterprise-wide financial planning, reporting, and strategic decision-making.

You will work closely with senior finance leaders and executive stakeholders, delivering insights that influence business performance at the highest level. This is an excellent opportunity for a CA-qualified professional looking to further develop their commercial acumen within a dynamic, fast-paced global environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead the preparation, consolidation, and ongoing enhancement of Group financial reporting templates
Deliver monthly Group reporting, including variance analysis and performance insights for leadership teams
Support the Group budgeting and forecasting process, consolidating inputs across regions
Provide financial modelling and analysis to support strategic initiatives and product innovation
Drive continuous improvement across reporting processes and data structures, leveraging ERP/EPM systems
Key Requirements:

CA qualified with 4–6 years’ experience in accounting, FP&A, or commercial finance
Background in a Group FP&A role within a listed organisation (ASX, NZX, or global equivalent)
Strong financial modelling, reporting, and analytical capabilities
Advanced Excel skills and experience working with large datasets
Exposure to ERP/EPM systems (Oracle highly regarded)
Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage and influence senior stakeholders
How to Apply

Job Title: Internal Audit General Manager - Tech & Info Security
Ad Text:
