One of Australia’s leading financial institutions, with a strong presence across retail, commercial, and institutional banking, operating in a highly regulated and complex environment.

Responsibility: 

Lead the technology and information security audit portfolio, including risk assessment, audit planning, and delivery of bank-wide audit activity aligned to the overall risk profile.
Provide subject matter expertise across technology, cyber, and data management risk, including developing methodologies, responding to emerging risks, and driving continuous improvement in audit practices.
Deliver risk-based audit coverage across technology functions and support integrated audits across broader business areas, ensuring consistent and high-quality outcomes.
Act as a key point of contact for senior stakeholders, influencing improvements in control environments and aligning audit insights with strategic priorities.

Strong experience within internal audit or second-line risk, with exposure to regulatory expectations (e.g. cyber, operational resilience, outsourcing), alongside a leadership style focused on accountability, delivery, and building high-performing teams.

Deep expertise in technology and information security risk within large, regulated environments, including oversight of enterprise cyber risk, control frameworks, and governance.

For over 240 years combined, Berry Street and Yooralla have been at the forefront of supporting Australians with a disability and those experiencing poverty, violence and abuse. Now, as one unified organisation, we bring together unparalleled expertise and a shared commitment to empowering individuals and families to thrive, while upholding and championing their human rights. We believe in a future where everyone has the opportunity to live the fulfilling life they choose for themselves, free from the impacts of trauma, violence, and abuse, achieved safely and with dignity.

The Opportunity

We’re seeking a highly capable Senior Technical Delivery Lead to guide a major identity transformation program. This role is perfect for someone who thrives at the intersection of security architecture, vendor management and strategic decision‑making. If you enjoy shaping complex solutions, influencing outcomes, this is your opportunity to make a significant impact!

Key Responsibilities

Act as the primary technical counterpart to our external delivery partner.
Coordinate discovery activities and ensure the right internal stakeholders are engaged.
Drive progress where requirements are evolving or not fully defined.
Monitor vendor delivery quality, cadence, and alignment with organisational priorities.
Prioritise and manage project scope and delivery in partnership with the delivery partner.
Interpret architecture designs across identity, infrastructure, and applications to inform decisions.
Ensure identity design choices consider impacts across cybersecurity, infrastructure, platforms, applications, and business domains.
Coordinate and facilitate technical and operational decisions throughout the project.
Support transition planning from implementation to steady‑state operations.
Engage with stakeholders across the business, from frontline staff to senior leaders.
Translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable outcomes for non‑technical stakeholders.
Support workshops, decision forums, and discovery activities with confidence and clarity.

About You

Strong understanding and experience of identity architecture, IAM platforms, authentication/authorisation patterns, and integration approaches.
Experience working across hybrid environments (cloud + on‑prem), including JML processes and legacy system constraints.
Ability to interpret architecture diagrams and translate them into practical design and delivery decisions.
Confident engaging with cybersecurity, infrastructure, application owners, business leaders, and external partners.
Able to translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical audiences.
Proactive, resilient, and comfortable navigating organisational ambiguity.

What You'll Receive

Coaching, reflective supervision and professional growth
Salary packaging (up to $15,900 + meals/accommodation)
Employee Assistance Program and well-being supports
A team that has your back and a culture that genuinely cares

Your new team

At Auto & General (A&G), we're a rapidly growing insurance business committed to safeguarding a brighter future for our customers, people, and community. We foster a high-performance culture built on integrity, collaboration, and dedication, known as the A&G Way. As an individual contributor in our Group Risk, Operational Risk division, you'll be part of a team that champions effective data management, governance, and quality practices across the organisation. As a leader, you'll model our values, drive high performance, and embody a One Team mindset.

The Role:
We're seeking a Senior Manager, Data Risk to provide crucial Line 2 oversight and challenge to data management, governance, and quality practices across the Auto & General Group. Based in Toowong or Melbourne, you will ensure our data-related risk controls align with regulatory, legislative, and industry best practices. This individual contributor role involves partnering with data owners, technology teams, and governance functions to enhance enterprise-wide practices, uplift our data strategy, and ensure regulatory compliance, ultimately supporting broader business objectives.


Position Accountabilities 

As Senior Manager, Data Risk, you will be accountable for:

Line 2 Risk Oversight:

Providing independent oversight and effective challenge to data risk management practices and programs.
Monitoring adherence to enterprise-wide data governance, quality standards, frameworks, and controls.
Reviewing and assessing material data risk exposure across Line 1 initiatives, including transformation projects, cloud migrations, and third-party arrangements.
Advising on risk implications of AGIC's data strategies, ensuring alignment with enterprise risk framework and business objectives.
Data Governance Frameworks:

Leading the embedding of the enterprise data risk framework, aligned with regulatory requirements and industry best practices.
Partnering with Line 1 to promote consistent application of data ownership models, metadata management practices, and stewardship frameworks.
Ensuring data risk management practices align with regulatory and legislative requirements, and internal policies.
Supporting regulatory reviews and audits, advising on data governance, reporting, and risk alignment for third-party data services.
Risk Assessments, Reporting and Insights:

Conducting thematic reviews and deep dives into critical data areas.
Providing detailed insights on emerging risks, ensuring these are reflected in risk profiles and action plans.
Delivering high-quality insights and commentary on data risk exposures, control effectiveness, and remediation progress to senior management and governance committees.
Tracking and reporting on risk issues, control gaps, and risk acceptances, providing metrics that measure the impact and progress of data governance efforts.

 

Qualifications & Experience:

7-10 years of experience in enterprise data governance and data risk, preferably within a regulated financial services or insurance environment.
Proven experience in Line 2 risk, assurance, or internal audit functions is highly desirable.
Expertise in data lifecycle management, data architecture principles, and regulatory compliance obligations.
High-level leadership and engagement skills, with the ability to manage SME experts the A&G way.
Excellent communication, influencing, and negotiation skills.
Ability to build effective relationships with senior managers and other key stakeholders.
Experience in the general insurance industry is highly regarded.
Qualifications:
Tertiary qualifications in Information Management, Data Governance, or related disciplines.
Professional certifications preferred: CDMP, CIMP, or related data governance and risk credentials.

Our Perks 


Location – Toowong

Close to a variety of public transport and easy parking options, the Toowong office has excellent end of trip facilities (including secure bike storage and showers), outdoor spaces and plenty of modern collaborative areas to work and connect with colleagues. 

Conveniently based at Toowong Village tower with views of the mountains and the Brisbane River, our location is only 4 km from the city and provides access to retail, restaurants and other amenities.

 


The benefits of working at A&G

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Work flexibility - with options to work from home two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone. 
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.

About us

Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community.  

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Qantas, Virgin Money and Coles Insurance.

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.

About the Organisation
Join a global investment management firm with a strong focus on long-term, responsible investment and sustainable value creation. The organisation operates across Australia, Europe, North America, and Asia, offering a high-performing, international environment. This role sits within a People & Culture team driving significant enterprise-wide capability initiatives and leadership development programs.

The Role
As L&D Consultant, you’ll focus on delivering and executing learning and capability initiatives across the enterprise. This is less about heavy instructional design and more about ensuring programs are embedded effectively, facilitated professionally, and aligned to the organisation’s culture and strategy.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading and supporting enterprise-wide learning and capability projects
Conducting learning needs analysis and translating findings into practical solutions
Managing vendors and procurement processes, including legal coordination
Facilitating workshops on performance reviews, development planning, and feedback conversations
Refreshing and contextualising vendor-provided content to align with organisational culture

Collaborating effectively across global time zones and with senior stakeholders

Strong project management skills and ability to manage multiple workstreams
High-level stakeholder management capability with credibility at senior levels
Experience in learning needs analysis and program execution
Vendor management and procurement experience, including legal processes
Facilitation skills for leadership and development sessions

Practical Details
Contract Duration: 6 months (Melbourne-based)

Job Title: Customer Engineering Manager, Data Analytics, Google Cloud
Ad Text:
Applicants must have a right to work in Australia as Google is currently unable to sponsor a visa for this position.

At Google, we have a vision of empowerment and equitable opportunity for all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and commit to building reconciliation through Google’s technology, platforms and people and we welcome Indigenous applicants. Please see our Reconciliation Action Plan for more information.

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Sydney NSW, Australia; Melbourne VIC, Australia.Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
10 years of experience with cloud native architecture in a customer-facing or support role.

Experience in pre-sales or field engineering at an enterprise technology company, or similar customer-facing experience.
Experience engaging with, and presenting to, technical stakeholders and executive leaders.
Experience in pre-sales management or people management on a data analytics-related or technical team.
Experience with data analytics technologies or concepts, cloud, and on-premise technologies.
Preferred qualifications:

Experience influencing cross-functional teams (e.g., Product Management, Engineering, Sales), customers, and partners to impact business goals, customer experience, and customer expansion.
Experience tailoring and delivering compelling messages to audiences, asking strategic questions, and leading conversations that drive business opportunity.
Experience in Big Data, including analytics warehousing, data processing, data transformation, data governance, data migrations, ETL, ELT, SQL, NoSQL, performance or scalability optimizations, or batch versus streaming.
Ability to be a thought leader in data analytics and adjacent practice areas, specializing in technical sales and strategizing around customer and industry solutions.
About the job


When leading companies choose Google Cloud, it's a huge win for spreading the power of cloud computing globally. Once educational institutions, government agencies, and other businesses sign on to use Google Cloud products, you come in to facilitate making their work more productive, mobile, and collaborative. You listen and deliver what is most helpful for the customer. You assist fellow sales Googlers by problem-solving key technical issues for our customers. You liaise with the product marketing management and engineering teams to stay on top of industry trends and devise enhancements to Google Cloud products.

Specialist Customer Engineers seek to accelerate Google Cloud customer success by providing domain-specific technical expertise, while responsibly influencing products and solutions.

As a Customer Engineering (CE) Manager, you lead and deploy a team of subject matter experts responsible for working alongside our customers to provide trusted technical and solution advice to accelerate workload migration and remove technical impediments.

Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

Responsibilities

Lead a team of technical experts in a matrixed organization. Focus on talent strategy, assessing go-to-market readiness and gaps in CE preparedness, skills development, and opportunity coverage to deliver successful cloud transformation outcomes for customers and accelerate business goals.

Execute the technical goal and strategy for your region’s data analytics practice. Lead the broader region's data analytics technical community, to ensure sub-regional technical contributions, achieve scale through artifact and innovation sharing.

Influence cross-functional teams, including Product Management and Engineering, ensuring customer needs are represented in product roadmaps and new technology is incubated and scaled.

Lead technical engagements with strategic customers, working with cross-functional peers to plan customer engagements.

Travel up to 25% to customer sites, conferences, and other related events as required, acting as a public advocate for Google Cloud.

Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. About OccuMED

OccuMED is a fast-growing healthcare business delivering occupational medicine services across Western Australia. We are entering an exciting new phase of growth, underpinned by investment in technology, system transformation, a focus on operational excellence and client service.

This role is a key leadership position within that journey — an opportunity to shape how finance operates in a modern, data-driven healthcare business.

The business is well established with strong revenues and a long history of positive cashflow.

The Opportunity

We are looking for an up-and-coming Finance Manager who is ready to step into a broader leadership role.

This is not a traditional “steady-state” finance role — it is hands-on, commercial, and transformation-focused. You will report and work closely with the CEO and leadership team to build capability, improve processes, and deliver meaningful financial insights that drive business performance.

You will lead and be accountable for the finance function, managing a small team to support this scaling organisation.

Key Responsibilities

Finance Leadership & Team Management

Lead, mentor and develop a small finance team across finance operations

Drive accountability, structure, and capability uplift within the team

Oversee payroll processes to ensure accuracy, compliance, and effective controls

Financial Operations & Systems Transformation

Lead migration from Reckon to Xero

Improve accuracy, timeliness, and efficiency of financial workflows

Reporting & Commercial Insights

Own monthly business performance reporting (labour ratios / operational metrics)

Monthly tax and compliance reporting

Establish daily dashboard reporting

Own cashflow forecasting and working capital management

Lead annual budgeting and forecast models

Oversee debtor management and collections performance

About You

We are looking for someone who is ready to step up, not someone who has been sitting in a long-tenured leadership role.

Ideal Profile:

5–10 years’ experience in a Finance Manager / Senior Accountant / Commercial Analyst role

Strong Xero capability (or proven experience transitioning systems)

Advanced Excel / financial modelling skills

Highly hands-on and operationally minded

Strong commercial acumen — you understand what drives business performance

A natural leader who wants to develop and lead a team

Energised by change, growth, and building something together

Why Join OccuMED?

Be part of a high-growth healthcare business

Work directly with an experienced leadership team, including seasoned operators who have successfully delivered growth and transformation playbooks, offering a fast-track learning environment

Exposure to cutting-edge technology and AI workflows

Apply

If you’re looking for a role where you can step up, lead, and make a real impact, we’d love to hear from you.
